candidate_problems.text: Determine whether there are any object categories for which one identity criterion is privileged across nearly all explanatory purposes, which would limit the pluralist conclusion drawn from the ship case.
Clarify how conflicts between causal-historical, material, and institutional continuity should be resolved when practical systems require a single answer rather than criterion-relative plurality.
Test whether the claim 'no unique answer without a specified criterion' generalizes to personal identity, biological organisms, and legal artifacts, or breaks in some adjacent domains.
